text.title: Laravel Umbrella Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed in real Laravel 10/11 production deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Endpoint latency spikes whenever a "background" job fires; CPU saturates the web pod under load.
antipattern.root-cause: `QUEUE_CONNECTION=sync` in production env executes jobs inline on the request thread.
antipattern.fix: Switch to `redis` (with Horizon) or `sqs`. Run `php artisan queue:work` under Supervisor. Verify via `php artisan queue:monitor`.

antipattern.symptom: Secrets leak to public GitHub mirror; access tokens revoked emergency at 03:00.
antipattern.root-cause: `.env` accidentally committed; `.gitignore` did not cover it.
antipattern.fix: Add `.env` + `.env.*` to `.gitignore`. Rotate all secrets. Store production secrets in 1Password / Vault and template into `.env` at deploy time.

antipattern.symptom: Scheduled tasks stop running silently after the worker crashes; no alert fires.
antipattern.root-cause: `php artisan schedule:work` ran in a tmux session; the host rebooted; process never restarted.
antipattern.fix: Run scheduler + queue workers under Supervisor with `autostart=true`, `autorestart=true`. Add a heartbeat job that pings dead-man's-snitch.

antipattern.symptom: Production runs on PHP 7.4; security patches stopped landing two years ago.
antipattern.root-cause: Composer `require` floor too low; deploys never enforced.
antipattern.fix: Set `"php": "^8.2"` in `composer.json`. Enforce via CI matrix that fails on lower versions.
